entry.title: Review: Wendy's - Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion
entry.summary:
Wendy's Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion features a Vanilla (or Chocolate) Frosty swirled with new snickerdoodle sauce and topped with snickerdoodle cookie crumbles.
It cost me $4.19 for a small, and I went with the vanilla version.
Unlike a McFlurry or a Blizzard, the Frosty Fusion is only mixed for a few seconds (if that). The sauce remained mostly drizzled on the sides and bottom of the cup, resulting in a mild cinnamon note to the creamy Vanilla Frosty and a much sweeter, more pronounced cinnamon taste whenever the sauce was encountered. Interestingly, the sauce contained visible flecks of cinnamon. The snickerdoodle cookie crumbles on top had the requisite mix of sweet vanilla and cinnamon flavors. They were super crunchy (although this experience may vary), providing a nice contrast to the smooth Frosty and liquid sauce.
Overall, I enjoyed Wendy's Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion a good deal. I like snickerdoodles, and the flavor was captured well here. I especially liked the hearty crunch of the cookies on top as that can be hit-or-miss with this type of dessert. Nutritional Info - Wendy's Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion Calories - 410 Fat - 11g (Saturated Fat - 6g) Sodium - 320mg Carbs - 70g (Sugar - 56g) Protein - 9g Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: McRib Returns to Select McDonald's Restaurants
entry.summary:
It's McRib season once again at McDonald's, but you can only find the barbecue sauce-slathered pork patty at participating locations in select regions.
Cities where the McRib is available for a limited time include Miami, Dallas, Cleveland, St. Louis, Atlanta, Chicago, Seattle, Los Angeles, and more. The McRib features a seasoned boneless pork patty shaped like a rack of ribs and slathered in barbecue sauce and served with slivered onions and pickles on a toasted cornmeal-dusted roll. Prices vary, but the McRib costs $6.29 at my local McDonald's in Los Angeles. The combo, which includes the sandwich, medium fries, and a medium drink, costs $11.29. Photo via McDonald's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Shake Shack's 2025 Holiday Shakes Arrive
entry.summary:
Shake Shack introduces two seasonal shakes for the 2025 holiday season: the Christmas Cookie Shake and the Sticky Toffee Pudding Shake.
Available for a limited time, the shakes are inspired by traditional holiday desserts. The returning Christmas Cookie Shake features a sweet, buttery frozen custard blended with milk and topped with whipped cream and holiday sprinkles. The new Sticky Toffee Pudding Shake features sticky toffee pudding (a type of steamed cake) spun with vanilla frozen custard and caramelized brown sugar, topped with whipped cream and a drizzle of sticky toffee pudding sauce. Prices vary, but both holiday shakes cost $6.99 each here in the Los Angeles area. Photo via Shake Shack. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Checkers & Rally's Introduces New $5 Pretzel Pubster Combo
entry.summary:
Checkers & Rally's launches its new $5 Pretzel Pubster Combo as the chain's latest value meal.
The new Pretzel Pubster Burger features two beef patties, melted beer cheese with bacon crumbles, American cheese, mayo, and crispy onions, served on a toasted pretzel bun. The $5 meal also includes Famous Seasoned Fries and a small fountain drink (the burger by itself costs $3.99 near me in the Los Angeles area). The fast-food chain also offers a bigger take on a pretzel burger in the new BBQ Pretzel Buford. With a suggested price of $7.49, the burger features two large beef patties, American cheese, two bacon strips, crispy-fried onions, Sweet & Smoky BBQ sauce, and mayo, on a toasted pretzel bun. Checkers & Rally's is also offering Classic Wings for $1 each for the fall sports season. The bone-in wings are available plain or tossed in one of several sauces, including new Frank's Stingin' Honey Garlic Sauce, Medium Buffalo Sauce, Angry Buffalo Sauce, Garlic Parmesan Sauce, and Sweet & Smoky BBQ. Photo via Checkers & Rally's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Del Taco Puts Together New $2.99 Micro Meal
entry.summary:
Calling it a "bite-sized meal deal that proves good things really do come in small packages," Del Taco has launched the new Micro Meal priced at $2.99.
Available for a limited time, the mini combo meal includes a Micro Beef & Cheddar Burrito, 7 to 10 Crinkle-Cut Fries, and a Donut Bite. The Micro Beef & Cheddar Burrito is a smaller version of the chain's Beef & Cheddar Burrito. It features seasoned beef, grated cheddar cheese, and zesty red sauce, wrapped in a warm flour tortilla. The value-priced meal is available now at participating Del Taco locations chain-wide through November 23, 2025. Photo via Del Taco. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Peppermint Chip is Back at Chick-fil-A
entry.summary:
Peppermint Chip has returned to the Chick-fil-A menu for the 2025 holiday season. Chicken Tortilla Soup is also back for the winter.
Chick-fil-A's returning Peppermint Chip lineup includes a trio of beverages: - Peppermint Chip Milkshake - Chick-fil-A's Icedream frozen dairy dessert blended with peppermint syrup and peppermint bark chips, topped with whipped cream and a maraschino cherry. - Peppermint Chip Frosted Coffee - Cold-brewed coffee, peppermint syrup, and peppermint bark chips blended with Icedream frozen dairy dessert. - Peppermint Iced Coffee - Cold-brewed coffee sweetened with pure cane syrup and peppermint syrup. Chicken Tortilla Soup is a winter staple for Chick-fil-A that features shredded chicken breast, navy beans, and black beans in a creamy white soup base with a blend of vegetables (including onions, corn, bell peppers, and celery) and spicy heat. It's served with seasoned corn tortilla strips for topping. Photo via Chick-fil-A. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: The Grand Papa Pizza Arrives at Papa Johns
entry.summary:
Papa Johns debuts its largest pizza yet with the launch of the new Grand Papa Pizza. The pizza chain also introduces a new dessert: the Salted Caramel Blondie.
The 18" XXL pizza is made with the chain's original dough that's been hand-stretched into a thin crust. It comes topped with new deli pepperoni, a 3-cheese blend, and Italian seasoning. It's priced at $14.99. Papa Johns describes its deli pepperoni as larger than regular pepperoni with a milder, more savory flavor. The new Salted Caramel Blondie is a warm, buttery blondie made with brown sugar, caramel chips, and a touch of sea salt. It's priced at $9.49, but is also available as part of the Papa Pairings deal, where you can select two or more menu items for $6.99 each. Photo via Papa Johns. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Crumbl Bakes New Dubai Chocolate Cheesecake and More Through November 15, 2025
entry.summary:
Crumbl introduces its new Dubai Chocolate Cheesecake and Chocolate Chip Cookie Thins as part of this week's rotation of cookies and desserts.
Here's the Crumbl menu for the week ending November 15, 2025: - New Dubai Chocolate Cheesecake (pictured) - A chocolate cheesecake on a chocolate graham crust, topped with a crispy kataifi and pistachio filling, a drizzle of pistachio cream, and a whipped cream rosette. - New Chocolate Chip Cookie Thins - Thin, crispy cookies with caramelized edges, chewy centers, and semi-sweet chocolate chips. - Caramel Apple Cookie - A warm cinnamon apple cookie topped with caramel cream cheese frosting, chopped Granny Smith apples, a caramel drizzle, and a house-made streusel. - S'mores Cookie - A warm graham cracker cookie packed with milk chocolate chips and topped with a melty marshmallow, chocolate drizzle, and buttery graham cracker crumbs. - Snickerdoodle Cookie - A warm vanilla sugar cookie rolled in cinnamon-sugar. - Mint Chip Ice Cream Cookie - A chilled sugar cookie loaded with semi-sweet chips, topped with a creamy mint chip mousse and a whipped cream rosette. - Strawberry Cake Cookie - A warm strawberry and vanilla cookie topped with strawberry cream cheese frosting and white chocolate curls. - Semi-Sweet Chocolate Chunk Cookie - A warm cookie packed with semi-sweet chocolate chunks and a sprinkle of flaky sea salt. Crumbl also continues to offer a weekly "secret menu" that's a dessert or cookie selected by each location. You can check each location's secret menu item on the Crumbl website or mobile app. Photo via Crumbl. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Domino's Offers Large 1-Topping Carryout Pizzas for $6.70 Each
entry.summary:
Spurred by a viral meme, Domino's is offering a limited-time, online, carryout deal where you can get large 1-topping pizzas for $6.70 each using the code "67".
The "67" (also "6-7") refers to a nonsensical meme that originated from the song "Doot Doot (6 7)" by Skrilla and gained popularity due to being featured on TikTok and Instagram Reels. It evolved into people just saying the phrase as slang with various meanings (or lack thereof), including as an inside joke where students cheer when they hear the words spoken (to the consternation of many a math teacher). The 67 deal compares well to Domino's regularly offered $7.99 large 1-topping carryout deal (it's an additional 17% discount) and is available through Sunday, November 16, 2025, at participating locations nationwide. In addition to keeping hip with the young folk with a viral deal, Domino's has just launched a rebrand that the pizza chain describes as, "We've taken inspiration from the past and present, and transformed it into modernized elements that will better reach future pizza lovers, including hotter colors, new packaging features a more simple, iconic approach and even a new jingle, "Dommmino’s," brought to life by hip-hop/country/Americana music star Shaboozey." You can see the changes to Domino's packaging, color palette, and more below:
According to the brand's consumer research and testing, the new look "significantly improved consumers' perceptions of the brand as more vibrant/bold, fun/playful and modern." Additionally, 67% of consumers preferred the new rebrand over the previous design, with 73% of Gen Z and 70% of Millennials in favor of the new look. Photos via Domino's. Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Review: McDonald's - Buffalo Ranch Sauce
entry.summary:
McDonald's new Buffalo Ranch sauce is described by the chain as "tangy meets creamy and mild-meets-wild as two iconic flavors come together to create something craveable."
I got the sauce with a six-piece order of Chicken McNuggets. The Buffalo Ranch had a yellow-orange color to it.
In terms of taste, it came off as a creamy Buffalo sauce. The ranch mostly contributed a creamy base and tang rather than any herby notes, which left the Buffalo sauce to add more tang as well as a light to medium heat. It went okay with the Chicken McNuggets, which have a more neutral profile compared to McDonald's McCrispy Strips. The tenders might have filled in better for the missing ranch notes.
Overall, McDonald's Buffalo Ranch isn't terribly exciting, but it saves you the trouble of dipping chicken in Buffalo sauce and then ranch (or vice versa). Read more at Brand Eating!

entry.title: Panera Introduces New Creme Brulee Bites
entry.summary:
In addition to the launch of its new Frozen Signature Hot Chocolate, Panera introduces new Creme Brulee Bites as a limited-time dessert that complements the Creme Brulee Latte, which debuted as part of this year's fall menu.
The Creme Brulee Bite is a mini buttery vanilla cake topped with brown sugar streusel crumb and salted caramel sugar. It can be purchased individually or in a pack of three and is similar in concept to the bakery-cafe chain's regular menu Brownie Bites (a mini fudge brownie topped with chocolate chips and brownie crumbs. Prices vary, but a Creme Brulee Bite costs $1.99 at my local Panera branch here in Los Angeles, while a 3-pack costs $5. Panera is also offering a limited-time $1 soup deal where you can get a cup of soup for $1 with an in-app purchase of a whole entree using the code "SOUP" at participating locations. Photo via Panera. Read more at Brand Eating!
